pi_items.source: task.json metadata field and confirmed by setup_task.sh line 8
Standard Bridge Command scenario file structure confirmed in export_result.sh parsing logic (lines 62-71) and environment-level data examples
task.json metadata and confirmed in setup_task.sh line 13 and export_result.sh line 6

Verified as valid Bridge Command setting through multiple references in task descriptions and confirmed 'Portsmouth' is a built-in setting (bridgecommand_env/data/portsmouth_approach/environment.ini). Solent is extensively referenced across multiple task descriptions suggesting it is an available setting.

Verified: Bridge Command weather scale is 0-12 (from https://www.bridgecommand.co.uk/Doc/4.7/Scenario.php). Value 3.0 is within valid range. Confirmed in environment.ini examples (e.g., portsmouth_approach/environment.ini shows Weather=3.0)

Verified: Visibility is measured in nautical miles in Bridge Command. Value 10.0 nm is realistic for daytime maritime conditions. Confirmed in portsmouth_approach/environment.ini which shows VisibilityRange=10.0
June (month 6) is a valid month for Solent maritime operations. Consistent with summer training schedule.

Verified: MV Celtic Mariner is a real General Cargo Ship built in 2010, currently flagged to Portugal. Confirmed via VesselFinder and MarineTraffic (https://www.vesselfinder.com/vessels/details/9377925)

Verified: Latitude 50.62°N is approximately 5.8 nautical miles south of Isle of Wight center (50.6752°N), matching task description 'south of Isle of Wight in open water'. Falls within verifier's accepted range: 50.5 ≤ lat ≤ 50.7
Verified: Longitude -1.15°W is in the Solent area (Isle of Wight: -1.2752°W). Falls within verifier's accepted range: -1.3 ≤ long ≤ -1.0. Represents realistic position for open water approach.
Verified: 12.0 knots is realistic operational speed for a General Cargo Ship. Verifier accepts range 10-15 knots (verifier.py line 106)
Verified: Heading 230° (SW direction) is valid for maritime navigation. Realistic approach heading for positioning in Solent operations.

Verified: Williamson Turn is a documented maritime maneuver used in Man Overboard recovery operations. Confirmed in MOB drill references from https://maritimeducation.com/ and https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Man_overboard_rescue_turn
Verified: PAN PAN is the standard maritime radio distress alert (second level, above all others except MAYDAY), used for urgent situations. Confirmed in maritime emergency communication standards.
Verified: 'Immediate Actions' is a standard section in MOB drill procedures. Documented in maritime safety training materials from https://maritimeducation.com/
Verified: MOB 'Recovery' procedures are standard in maritime drill documentation. Core element of Man Overboard recovery operations.

privileged_info_summary: The MOB Recovery Drill task is a synthetic simulation exercise using Bridge Command maritime simulator. Task creates a Man Overboard recovery training scenario in the Solent area (UK), using real geographic coordinates (50.62°N, -1.15°W) approximately 5.8 nm south of Isle of Wight. Key elements verified: (1) MV Celtic Mariner is a real General Cargo Ship (IMO 9377925, built 2010); (2) Environmental parameters are realistic—Weather scale 0-12 with value 3.0 (moderate), Visibility 10.0 nautical miles (good conditions); (3) Bridge Command settings confirmed—'Solent' is a valid setting used across multiple training scenarios, weather/visibility scales match documentation; (4) MOB drill procedure keywords (Williamson Turn, PAN PAN, Immediate Actions, Recovery) are authentic maritime safety terms; (5) Spatial geometry validated—coordinates within verifier acceptance thresholds (50.5-50.7°N, -1.3 to -1.0°W), vessel speed 12 knots realistic for cargo ship, heading 230° valid for approach maneuvers.
